title: Project canonical operator decision axis in Leitstand
goal: Add a read-only Leitstand decision-axis projection that compresses existing operator truth into Jetzt, Im Fokus, Blockiert, Konvergenz and Danach without inventing a new priority, task, runtime or convergence authority. Jetzt and Danach come from canonical Bureau ranking, Im Fokus from the Bureau Live Register, Blockiert from source-bound blocker evidence, and Konvergenz from existing convergence evidence. Leitstand must preserve its observer invariant and consume producer-side snapshots rather than calling Bureau or Grabowski in the request path.

approval.note: User explicitly authorized implementation on 2026-07-23. Preserve Leitstand as a read-only derived display and the existing producer-side snapshot boundary.

acceptance.assertion: A machine-readable producer-side snapshot exposes Jetzt, Im Fokus, Blockiert, Konvergenz and Danach with explicit source and freshness metadata and without assigning new priority or lifecycle semantics.

Jetzt and Danach are derived from canonical Bureau ranking, Im Fokus from the Live Register, Blockiert from bounded source-bound blocker evidence, and Konvergenz from existing convergence evidence; missing or stale evidence remains visibly degraded instead of being inferred green.
Leitstand performs no request-time calls to Bureau or Grabowski and gains no claim, dispatch, merge, deployment, task mutation or runtime mutation authority.
The dashboard or a dedicated operator view renders the five decision-axis sections clearly and source-links or discloses provenance and freshness without duplicating the full Bureau board.
Tests cover fresh complete data, missing or stale sources, no current eligible ball, bounded blockers and convergence states, and prove that the view remains read-only and snapshot-backed.
Existing Bureau task snapshots and convergence or operator artifacts are reused or extended; no second queue, focus registry or global status model is introduced.

does_not_establish: permission to mutate foreign dirty worktrees
